key_decisions_and_rationale: Provider key is omni while model id remains native.vertex.gemini-omni-flash-preview, so scheduler ownership and model catalog identity are separate.
Omni runtime fails closed rather than falling back to fake provider output because 19400 is for real-provider testing only.
Automated tests use injected fake subprocesses only; runtime fake success for Omni is blocked.
The 720p display fix is UI-only: request parameters still omit unsupported Omni resolution while card metadata displays the provider output resolution.
The prompt-redaction change was made after code review to avoid public Omni prompts leaking through publicJob, but it may be the reason refreshed cards now show No prompt provided.
Generated media preservation is not a git merge concern; it depends on keeping the shared NATIVE_MEDIA_ROOT or copying specific job/asset records by job id.
issues_discovered: Initial agent mistakenly did a web lookup despite audited plan; Karim corrected course. Do not do further web research unless explicitly asked or a concrete contradiction blocks implementation.
Code-reviewer found Omni prompts exposed through publicJob and gallery hydration; fixed by stripping prompt, parameters, and inputs from public Omni projections.
After that fix, Karim reported refreshed 19400 cards show No prompt provided for new Omni generations. This is unresolved and should be investigated read-only.
Next dev logs include existing warnings about conflicting star exports for isNativeModelId in packages/studio/src/index.js; this was not part of the Omni fix.
During one Next restart, asset proxy requests showed terminated/socket errors because the process was being stopped while the UI was loading assets; not treated as a code issue.
Startup reconciliation after restart reported unknown: 6 and unchanged: 106; investigate only if it affects Karim's current media visibility.
